channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: At least 30 people killed by Israeli strikes in Gaza as stalled ceasefire talks set to resume
item.description: Hospital staff say at least 30 people, including children, were killed in Gaza by Israeli strikes overnight and Friday morning, as air sirens sounded across Israel and stalled ceasefire talks were set to resume.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa And Sam Mednick, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Korea's impeached president defies warrant after hourslong standoff
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean investigators left the official residence of impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol after a near-six-hour standoff on Friday during which he defied their attempt to detain him.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: German and French foreign ministers seek 'new beginning' with Syria
item.description: The German and French foreign ministers are heading to Damascus to send what the German minister said is a clear signal that a “political new beginning” between Europe and Syria is possible.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes across Gaza kill at least 50, including several kids
item.description: Israeli airstrikes killed at least 50 people across central and southern Gaza, including an attack on a sprawling tent camp that Israel has repeatedly bombed despite designating it a humanitarian safe zone.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: South Korean investigators attempt to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean investigators arrived at the presidential residence with a warrant to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol over his short-lived imposition of martial law, but faced resistance from presidential security staff as hundreds of Yoon’s supporters gathered outsid
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Duchess of Sussex's Netflix series set for January premiere
item.description: Meghan, the Duchess of Sussex, has a Netflix series set for a mid-January premiere, the streaming service said in a Thursday post on the social platform X. The show, titled “With Love, Meghan,” starts streaming Jan. 15.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Puerto Rico's new governor sworn in days after a major blackout left much of the island in the dark
item.description: Jenniffer González Colón has been sworn in as Puerto Rico’s new governor as the island held for a normally ebullient ceremony overshadowed by widespread anger over a blackout that hit the U.S. territory days ago.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bangladesh court again rejects bail for Hindu leader who led rallies
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — A court in southeastern Bangladesh on Thursday rejected a plea for bail by a jailed Hindu leader who led large rallies in the Muslim-majority country demanding better security for minority groups.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: British rapper Stormzy banned from driving for using phone behind wheel of his Rolls-Royce
item.description: British rapper Stormzy has been banned from driving for nine months after admitting that he drove his Rolls-Royce through London while using his phone.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Montenegro mourns after gunman kills at least 12 people before shooting himself
item.description: Shock and dismay prevails in Montenegro after a gunman fatally shot 12 people, including two children, in a western town before killing himself.
item.dc:creator: Predrag Milic, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 12 in Gaza, including children, as war grinds into the new year
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli strikes killed at least 12 Palestinians in the Gaza Strip, mostly women and children, officials said Wednesday, as the nearly 15-month war ground on into the new year.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Power is restored to nearly all of Puerto Rico after a major blackout
item.description: BAYAMÓN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Power was restored to nearly all electrical customers across Puerto Rico on Wednesday after a sweeping blackout plunged the U.S. territory into darkness on New Year’s Eve.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA PÉREZ PINTADO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The world welcomes 2025 with light shows, embraces and ice plunges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — From Sydney to Mumbai to Paris to Rio de Janeiro, communities around the world welcomed 2025 with spectacular light shows, embraces and ice plunges.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, CEDAR ATTANASIO and CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: German chancellor takes veiled swipe at Elon Musk in New Year's address
item.description: Scholz's comments come days after the German government accused the billionaire of trying to influence its February election.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, NewsNation

item.title: Pope calls for protection of life in New Year's message
item.description: Pope Francis called for the protection of life at New Year’s Day Mass on Wednesday, offering a seemingly anti-abortion message. “Let us entrust this new year to Mary, Mother of God,” Francis said in his homily at Saint Peter’s Basilica.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine stops transport of Russian gas to Europe
item.description: Ukraine has halted the transportation of Russian gas supplies through the country after a prewar transit deal expired at the end of last year, the nation's energy minister confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, The Hill

item.title: South Korea air crash investigators extract black box data as grieving families mourn the victims
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Grieving relatives of the victims of the South Korea plane crash gathered at the site to pay respects to their loved ones on New Year’s Day, as officials said they've extracted data from one of the retrieved black boxes to find the exact cause of the crash.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden speaks to Puerto Rico governor, Energy secretary about power outages
item.description: President Biden said he spoke with the governor of Puerto Rico and the Energy secretary after a blackout hit nearly all of the island early Tuesday morning. During their talk, Biden offered Puerto Rico Gov.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Nearly all of Puerto Rico is without power on New Year's Eve
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — A blackout hit nearly all of Puerto Rico early on Tuesday as the U.S. territory prepared to celebrate New Year’s, leaving more than 1.3 million clients in the dark. Officials said it could take up to two days to restore power.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: France's Macron acknowledges snap elections backfired
item.description: France’s President Emmanuel Macron admitted that his decision to hold parliamentary elections early caused instability in New Year's Eve comments.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Migrants accused of killing Mexican border agent after he asked for their IDs: officials
item.description: Three suspected migrants were arrested Monday for allegedly killing a Mexican immigration agent who had asked to see their identification documents, Mexican officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The year in review: Influential people who died in 2024
item.description: It was a life that took him from peanut farming to the presidency. While former President Jimmy Carter ’s time in the White House only lasted one term, the decades afterward were defined by humanitarian work that affected people all around the world.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD McGHEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Airline says pre-flight inspection of South Korea plane showed 'no issues': report
item.description: Investigators are in South Korea searching through plane crash debris to try to determine what caused the country's deadliest air disaster in decades, which killed 179.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A new year dawns on a Middle East torn by conflict and change
item.description: DAMASCUS (AP) — In Damascus, the streets were buzzing with excitement Tuesday as Syrians welcomed in a new year that seemed to many to bring a promise of a brighter future after the unexpected fall of Bashar Assad’s government weeks earlier.
item.dc:creator: GHAITH ALSAYED, WAFAA SHURAFA and LUJAIN JO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Running a marathon a day in 2024, she has a world record within her grasp
item.description: GHENT, Belgium (AP) — Finally coming to a halt on Tuesday — the last day of 2024 — Belgian ultra runner Hilde Dosogne felt she had done all to take the world record as the first woman to run a marathon every single day of the year.
item.dc:creator: RAF CASERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australia, New Zealand first to welcome 2025 with fireworks
item.description: The U.S. countdown is still hours away, but those in New Zealand have already started 2025 with a bang.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Longest-held Guantánamo Bay detainee sent back to Tunisia
item.description: A detainee at a military prison in Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, has been repatriated to Tunisia, the Pentagon announced Monday. Ridah Bin Saleh al-Yazidi, 59, was eligible to transfer following an interagency review process, the release said. He was the longest-held detainee at the prison.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: South Korea to inspect Boeing aircraft as it struggles to find cause of plane crash that killed 179
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean officials said Monday they will conduct safety inspections of all Boeing 737-800 aircraft operated by the country's airlines, as they struggle to determine what caused a plane crash that killed 179 people a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Serbian court convicts the parents of a teenage boy who shot dead 10 people in a school in Belgrade
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — A court in Serbia on Monday convicted the parents of a teenage boy who last year shot dead nine pupils and a school guard and wounded six more people in a school in central Belgrade.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin offers to pay off debts as recruitment tool in war against Ukraine
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin signed a bill into law last month that offers to pay off up to $96,000 in debt in exchange for military service.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about H-1B visas at the center of Musk-MAGA fight
item.description: A simmering debate over work visas exploded over the last days of the year, exposing a rift over immigration among President-elect Trump’s closest supporters.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Uncovering the atrocities of the Assad regime at its 'death factory' on the hill
item.description: Sednaya prison was the site where thousands of Syrians were tortured and killed under Assad’s regime. Investigators have uncovered mass graves, and are calling for justice.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US sends Ukraine final Biden-approved weapons, financial package
item.description: The Biden administration's final aid to Ukraine before Donald Trump takes office is a $1.25 billion drawdown package.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Jetliner skids off runway and bursts into flames while landing in South Korea, killing 179
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A jetliner skidded off a runway, slammed into a concrete fence and burst into flames Sunday in South Korea after its landing gear apparently failed to deploy. All but two of the 181 people aboard were killed in one of the country’s worst aviation disasters, officials said.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Azerbaijan's president says crashed jetliner was shot down by Russia unintentionally
item.description: Azerbaijan’s President Ilham Aliyev said Sunday that the Azerbaijani airliner that crashed last week was shot down by Russia, albeit unintentionally, and criticized Moscow for trying to “hush up” the issue for days. "We can say with complete clarity that the plane was shot down by Russia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US teacher jailed in Russia is formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than three years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained, the State Department said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: China sanctions 7 companies over US military assistance to Taiwan
item.description: The sanctions also come in response to the recent approval of the U.S. government's annual defense spending bill, which a Chinese Foreign Ministry statement said “includes multiple negative sections on China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation
